section.heading: Chief Financial Officer of the Intelligence Community

subsection.heading: Chief Financial Officer of the Intelligence Community

content.p: To assist the Director of National Intelligence in carrying out the responsibilities of the Director under this chapter and other applicable provisions of law, there is within the Office of the Director of National Intelligence a Chief Financial Officer of the Intelligence Community who shall be appointed by the Director. The Chief Financial Officer shall report directly to the Director of National Intelligence.

subsection.heading: Duties and responsibilities
subsection.chapeau: Subject to the direction of the Director of National Intelligence, the Chief Financial Officer of the Intelligence Community shall—

paragraph.content: serve as the principal advisor to the Director of National Intelligence and the Principal Deputy Director of National Intelligence on the management and allocation of intelligence community budgetary resources;
participate in overseeing a comprehensive and integrated strategic process for resource management within the intelligence community;

paragraph.chapeau: ensure that the strategic plan of the Director of National Intelligence—

subparagraph.content: is based on budgetary constraints as specified in the Future Year Intelligence Plans and Long-term Budget Projections required under section 3103 of this title; and
contains specific goals and objectives to support a performance-based budget;

paragraph.content: prior to the obligation or expenditure of funds for the acquisition of any major system pursuant to a Milestone A or Milestone B decision, receive verification from appropriate authorities that the national requirements for meeting the strategic plan of the Director have been established, and that such requirements are prioritized based on budgetary constraints as specified in the Future Year Intelligence Plans and the Long-term Budget Projections for such major system required under section 3103 of this title;

ensure that the collection architectures of the Director are based on budgetary constraints as specified in the Future Year Intelligence Plans and the Long-term Budget Projections required under section 3103 of this title;
coordinate or approve representations made to Congress by the intelligence community regarding National Intelligence Program budgetary resources;
participate in key mission requirements, acquisitions, or architectural boards formed within or by the Office of the Director of National Intelligence; and
perform such other duties as may be prescribed by the Director of National Intelligence.

content.p: The Chief Financial Officer of the Intelligence Community shall serve as the Chief Financial Officer of the intelligence community and, to the extent applicable, shall have the duties, responsibilities, and authorities specified in chapter 9 of title 31.

subsection.heading: Prohibition on simultaneous service as other Chief Financial Officer

content.p: An individual serving in the position of Chief Financial Officer of the Intelligence Community may not, while so serving, serve as the chief financial officer of any other department or agency, or component thereof, of the United States Government.

paragraph.content: The term “major system” has the meaning given that term in section 3097(e) of this title.
The term “Milestone A” has the meaning given that term in section 3103(f) 11 So in original. Section 3103 of this title does not contain a subsec. (f). of this title.
The term “Milestone B” has the meaning given that term in section 3099(e) of this title.

section.sourceCredit: (July 26, 1947, ch. 343, title I, § 103I, as added Pub. L. 111–259, title IV, § 406(a), Oct. 7, 2010, 124 Stat. 2720; amended Pub. L. 116–92, div. E, title LXIV, § 6404, Dec. 20, 2019, 133 Stat. 2196.)

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: This chapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “this Act”, meaning act July 26, 1947, ch. 343, 61 Stat. 495, known as the National Security Act of 1947, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 403–3i of this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2019—Subsec. (a). Pub. L. 116–92 inserted at end “The Chief Financial Officer shall report directly to the Director of National Intelligence.”
